2604.24700.title: Green Shielding: A User-Centric Approach Towards Trustworthy AI
2604.24700.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed, yet their outputs can be highly sensitive to routine, non-adversarial variation in how users phrase queries, a gap not well addressed by existing red-teaming efforts. We propose Green Shielding, a user-centric agenda for building evidence-backed deployment guidance by characterizing how benign input variation shifts model behavior. We operationalize this agenda through the CUE criteria: benchmarks with authentic Context, reference standards and metrics that capture true Utility, and perturbations that reflect realistic variations in the Elicitation of model behavior. Guided by the PCS framework and developed with practicing physicians, we instantiate Green Shielding in medical diagnosis through HealthCareMagic-Diagnosis (HCM-Dx), a benchmark of patient-authored queries, together with structured reference diagnosis sets and clinically grounded metrics for evaluating differential diagnosis lists. We also study perturbation regimes that capture routine input variation and show that prompt-level factors shift model behavior along clinically meaningful dimensions. Across multiple frontier LLMs, these shifts trace out Pareto-like tradeoffs. In particular, neutralization, which removes common user-level factors while preserving clinical content, increases plausibility and yields more concise, clinician-like differentials, but reduces coverage of highly likely and safety-critical conditions. Together, these results show that interaction choices can systematically shift task-relevant properties of model outputs and support user-facing guidance for safer deployment in high-stakes domains. Although instantiated here in medical diagnosis, the agenda extends naturally to other decision-support settings and agentic AI systems.
2604.24700.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地被部署，但它們的輸出對用戶提問方式的常規、非對抗性變化非常敏感，而這一差距在現有的紅隊工作中並未得到充分解決。<lb/>我們提出了綠色防護（Green Shielding），這是一個以用戶為中心的議程，旨在通過描述良性輸入變化如何改變模型行為來建立基於證據的部署指導。<lb/>我們通過CUE標準來實現這一議程：具有真實上下文的基準，捕捉真實效用的參考標準和指標，以及反映模型行為引出過程中現實變化的擾動。<lb/>在PCS框架的指導下，並與執業醫生共同開發，我們在醫療診斷中實現了綠色防護，通過HealthCareMagic-Diagnosis（HCM-Dx），這是一個患者撰寫的查詢基準，並配有結構化的參考診斷集和臨床基礎的指標來評估鑑別診斷清單。<lb/>我們還研究了捕捉常規輸入變化的擾動規範，並顯示提示級因素在臨床上有意義的維度上改變模型行為。<lb/>在多個前沿LLM中，這些變化描繪出類似帕累托的權衡。<lb/>特別是，中和（neutralization）去除了常見的用戶級因素，同時保留了臨床內容，增加了合理性並產生了更簡潔、類似臨床醫生的鑑別，但減少了對高度可能和安全關鍵條件的覆蓋。<lb/>總體而言，這些結果顯示，交互選擇可以系統性地改變模型輸出的任務相關屬性，並支持用戶面向的指導，以便在高風險領域進行更安全的部署。<lb/>儘管在這裡具體應用於醫療診斷，但該議程自然擴展到其他決策支持環境和自主AI系統。
2604.24700.author: Aaron J. Li et.al.
2604.24700.authors: Aaron J. Li, Nicolas Sanchez, Hao Huang, Ruijiang Dong, Jaskaran Bains, Katrin Jaradeh, Zhen Xiang, Bo Li, Feng Liu, Aaron Kornblith, Bin Yu
